name: Åland Islands (1922)

colors.symbolism: the outer bands; the blue of Sweden and of Åland's heritage
the central band; the yellow of Sweden and of Åland's coat of arms

description: A horizontal triband of blue, yellow, and blue.
history: An early, unofficial flag flown in Åland from 1922, when many Ålanders, fearing the loss of their Swedish identity after Finland's independence, favoured colours recalling Sweden. The dominant version had three equal horizontal bands of blue-yellow-blue, though several variants existed. Such blue-and-yellow flags were banned by the Finnish government in 1935, and Åland eventually adopted its official red-cross-on-blue Nordic flag in 1954.

features.symbolism: the Swedish blue and yellow expressing Åland's Swedish cultural identity
